label: 5541 Definitions
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large , (d), (e).5 U.S.C. 901(a)§ 101(a), (d), (e), , 296.June 30, 1945, ch. 21259 Stat. 295, .Sept. 1, 1954, ch. 1208, § 202(a)68 Stat. 1109 (less clause (1) and last sentence of (a)).5 U.S.C. 902(less clause (1) and last sentence of (a)), .June 30, 1945, ch. 212, § 10259 Stat. 296, .May 24, 1946, ch. 270, § 8(a)60 Stat. 218, .Aug. 4, 1947, ch. 452, § 161 Stat. 727, .Aug. 18, 1959Pub. L. 86–168, § 202(c)73 Stat. 389 (as applicable to the Federal Employees Pay Act of 1945, as amended).5 U.S.C. 2358(a), (as applicable to the Federal Employees Pay Act of 1945, as amended), .July 17, 1959Pub. L. 86–91, § 10(a)73 Stat. 217The section is revised as a definition section. The provisions of former section 901(d) are omitted as unnecessary because the sections referred to state their application and there is no need to restate the application here.In paragraph (1), the terms “Executive agency” and “military department” are substituted for the references in former section 901(a) and (e) to the executive branch, including Government-owned or controlled corporations, and the General Accounting Office in view of the definitions in sections 105 and 102.In paragraph (2)(iii), the words “chapter 15 of title 31, District of Columbia Code” are substituted for the reference in former section 902(a)(4) to “the Teachers Salary Act of , as amended” on authority of the provisions contained therein. Enumeration of the individuals to which the provisions apply are added.June 4, 1924In paragraph (2)(iv), the provisions of former section 902(a)(5) and (b)(6) are combined.In paragraph (2)(v), the words “student-employee as defined by ” are coextensive with and substituted for the enumeration of the employees in former section 902(a)(6).section 5351 of this titleIn paragraph (2)(iv), (vi), (vii), (viii), (ix), (xi), and (xii), the reference to former section 947 is omitted as that section was repealed by the , .Act of Sept. 12, 1950, ch. 946, § 301(85)64 Stat. 843In paragraph (2)(xii), the reference to former section 946 is omitted as unnecessary since that section is not carried into this subchapter. The words “Panama Canal Company” are substituted for “Panama Railroad Company” on authority of the , .Act of Sept. 2, 1950, ch. 1049, § 2(a)(2)64 Stat. 1038In paragraph (2)(xiii), the words “as defined by ” are added on authority of former section 2351, which section is scheduled for transfer to .section 901 of title 20section 901 of title 20The exception for officers and employees of the Inland Waterways Corporation in former section 902(b)(3) is omitted on authority of the Act of , , .July 19, 1963Pub. L. 88–6777 Stat. 81Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

(2)(C)(ix), is section 1(a) of , , which was classified to section 1291(a) of the former Appendix to Title 50, War and National Defense, prior to editorial reclassification as .Section 1291(a) of title 50act Mar. 24, 1943, ch. 2657 Stat. 45section 4701(a) of Title 50

Amendments2010—Par. (2)(iv)(II). struck out “a member of the United States Secret Service Uniformed Division,” before “a member of the United States Park Police”.Pub. L. 111–2822008—Par. (2)(xi). substituted “section 5544 or 5550b” for “section 5544”.Pub. L. 110–1811996—Par. (2)(xii). inserted “or” after “Services Administration,” and struck out “, or a vessel employee of the Panama Canal Commission” after “Interior”.Pub. L. 104–2011992—Par. (3). added par. (3).Pub. L. 102–3781990—Par. (2)(iv). amended cl. (iv) generally. Prior to amendment, cl. (iv) read as follows: “a member of the Metropolitan Police, the Fire Department of the District of Columbia, the United States Park Police, or the Executive Protective Service;”.Pub. L. 101–5091988—Par. (2)(xvii). added cl. (xvii).Pub. L. 100–3251980—Par. (2)(xiv). struck out “within the meaning of section 401 of the Foreign Service Act of 1946” after “officer”.Pub. L. 96–465Par. (2)(xv). substituted “a member of the Senior Foreign Service” for “a ‘Foreign Service information officer’ as provided for by the first section of the Act entitled ‘An Act to promote the foreign policy of the United States by strengthening and improving the Foreign Service personnel system of the International Communication Agency through establishment of a Foreign Service Information Officer Corps’, approved ”.Pub. L. 96–465August 20, 19681979—Par. (2)(xii). substituted “Commission” for “Company”.Pub. L. 96–701978—Par. (2)(xvi). added cl. (xvi).Pub. L. 95–454Par. (2)(xv). substituted “International Communication Agency” for “United States Information Agency”.Pub. L. 95–4261977—Par. (2)(xiv), (xv). added cls. (xiv) and (xv).Pub. L. 95–1051975—Par. (2)(iv). substituted “Executive Protective Service” for “White House Police”.Pub. L. 94–1831972—Par. L. 103–329, title VI, § 633(a)108 Stat. 2425Sept. 30, 1994“This section [enacting , amending sections 5542 and 5547 of this title and , Labor, and enacting provisions set out as notes under ] may be cited as the ‘Law Enforcement Availability Pay Act of 1994’.”section 5545a of this titlesection 213 of Title 29section 5545a of this title

Availability of Premium Pay for Attorneys Employed in Department of Justice, , , 1501A–21, provided that:Pub. L. 106–113, div. B, § 1000(a)(1) [title I, § 115]113 Stat. 1535Nov. 29, 1999“(a)None of the funds made available by this or any other Act may be used to pay premium pay under title 5, United States Code, sections 5542–5549, to any individual employed as an attorney, including an Assistant United States Attorney, in the Department of Justice for any work performed on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Nov. 29, 1999“(b)Notwithstanding any other provision of law, neither the United States nor any individual or entity acting on its behalf shall be liable for premium pay under title 5, United States Code, sections 5542–5549, for any work performed on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [] by any individual employed as an attorney in the Department of Justice, including an Assistant United States Attorney.”Nov. 29, 1999[, , , 2762A–68, provided that: ]Pub. L. 106–553, § 1(a)(2) [title I, § 111]114 Stat. 2762Dec. 21, 2000“Section 115 of the Departments of Commerce, Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2000 (as enacted into law by ) [set out above] shall apply hereafter.”section 1000(a)(1) of Public Law 106–113

Sense of Congress Relating to Law Enforcement Officer Provisions, , , provided that:Pub. L. 102–378, § 2(40)(D)106 Stat. 1351Oct. 2, 1992“It is the sense of the Congress that—“(i)the provisions of , United States Code (as added by section 2(40)(C) of this Act)—section 5541(3) of title 5“(I)are enacted only for the purposes of pay and not for the purposes of retirement;“(II)do not reflect any intent of the Congress to change retirement eligibility standards for law enforcement officers; and“(ii)law enforcement officers in primary positions have different retirement eligibility standards than employees in supervisory or administrative positions because of the different requirements in their responsibilities.”

Payment of Bonuses for Foreign Language Capabilities, , , as amended by , , , 1468, provided that:Pub. L. 100–690, title VI, § 6401102 Stat. 4370Pub. L. 101–509, title V, § 529 [title IV, § 408(c)]104 Stat. 1427Nov. 18, 1988Nov. 5, 1990“(a).—In GeneralNotwithstanding any other provision of law, the Drug Enforcement Administration and the Federal Bureau of Investigation are authorized on and after , to pay bonuses up to 25 percent of base pay to employees of the Drug Enforcement Administration and the Federal Bureau of Investigation who possess and make substantial use of one or more languages, other than English, in the performance of their official duties. The Administrator of the Drug Enforcement Administration and the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation shall develop such policies as necessary to implement the payment of these bonuses.October 1, 1988“(b).—LimitationThe provisions of this section shall apply only to an employee who has received a bonus under this section before . The provisions of subchapter III of chapter 45 of title 5, United States Code, shall apply to any employee who would otherwise be eligible to receive a bonus under this section, on and after such date.”January 1, 1992
Executive Documents

Transfer of FunctionsEnvironmental Science Services Administration in Department of Commerce, including offices of Administrator and Deputy Administrator thereof, abolished by Reorg. Plan No. 4 of 1970, eff. , 35 F.R. 15627, , set out in the Appendix to this title, which created National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration in Department of Commerce and transferred personnel, property, records, and unexpended balances of funds of Environmental Science Services Administration to such newly created National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. Components of Environmental Science Services Administration thus transferred included Weather Bureau [now National Weather Service], Coast and Geodetic Survey [now National Ocean Survey], Environmental Data Service, National Environmental Satellite Center, and ESSA Research Laboratories.Oct. 3, 197084 Stat. 2090

Sense of Congress Relating to Law Enforcement Officer Provisions, , , provided that:Pub. L. 102–378, § 2(40)(D)106 Stat. 1351Oct. 2, 1992“It is the sense of the Congress that—“(i)the provisions of , United States Code (as added by section 2(40)(C) of this Act)—section 5541(3) of title 5“(I)are enacted only for the purposes of pay and not for the purposes of retirement;“(II)do not reflect any intent of the Congress to change retirement eligibility standards for law enforcement officers; and“(ii)law enforcement officers in primary positions have different retirement eligibility standards than employees in supervisory or administrative positions because of the different requirements in their responsibilities.”

Payment of Bonuses for Foreign Language Capabilities, , , as amended by , , , 1468, provided that:Pub. L. 100–690, title VI, § 6401102 Stat. 4370Pub. L. 101–509, title V, § 529 [title IV, § 408(c)]104 Stat. 1427Nov. 18, 1988Nov. 5, 1990“(a).—In GeneralNotwithstanding any other provision of law, the Drug Enforcement Administration and the Federal Bureau of Investigation are authorized on and after , to pay bonuses up to 25 percent of base pay to employees of the Drug Enforcement Administration and the Federal Bureau of Investigation who possess and make substantial use of one or more languages, other than English, in the performance of their official duties. The Administrator of the Drug Enforcement Administration and the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation shall develop such policies as necessary to implement the payment of these bonuses.October 1, 1988“(b).—LimitationThe provisions of this section shall apply only to an employee who has received a bonus under this section before . The provisions of subchapter III of chapter 45 of title 5, United States Code, shall apply to any employee who would otherwise be eligible to receive a bonus under this section, on and after such date.”January 1, 1992

Transfer of FunctionsEnvironmental Science Services Administration in Department of Commerce, including offices of Administrator and Deputy Administrator thereof, abolished by Reorg. Plan No. 4 of 1970, eff. , 35 F.R. 15627, , set out in the Appendix to this title, which created National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration in Department of Commerce and transferred personnel, property, records, and unexpended balances of funds of Environmental Science Services Administration to such newly created National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. Components of Environmental Science Services Administration thus transferred included Weather Bureau [now National Weather Service], Coast and Geodetic Survey [now National Ocean Survey], Environmental Data Service, National Environmental Satellite Center, and ESSA Research Laboratories.Oct. 3, 197084 Stat. 2090

children.label: A
children.text: an Executive agency;

children.label: B
children.text: a military department;

children.label: C
children.text: an agency in the judicial branch;

children.label: D
children.text: the Library of Congress;

children.label: E
children.text: the Botanic Garden;

children.label: F
children.text: the Office of the Architect of the Capitol; and

children.label: G
children.text: the government of the District of Columbia;

children.label: A
children.text: an employee in or under an Executive agency;

children.label: B
children.text: an individual employed by the government of the District of Columbia; and

children.label: C
children.text: an employee in or under the judicial branch, the Library of Congress, the Botanic Garden, and the Office of the Architect of the Capitol, who occupies a position subject to chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of this title;

children.label: i
children.text: a justice or judge of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: the head of an agency other than the government of the District of Columbia;

children.label: iii
children.text: a teacher, school official, or employee of the Board of Education of the District of Columbia, whose pay is fixed under chapter 15 of title 31, District of Columbia Code;

children.label: I
children.text: the Metropolitan Police or the Fire Department of the District of Columbia; or

children.label: II
children.text: a member of the United States Park Police, other than for purposes of section 5545(a) and 5546;1So in original. Probably should be “sections”.1

children.label: v
children.text: a student-employee as defined by ;section 5351 of this title

children.label: vii
children.text: an employee outside the continental United States or in Alaska who is paid in accordance with local native prevailing wage rates for the area in which employed;

children.label: viii
children.text: an employee of the Tennessee Valley Authority;

children.label: ix
children.text: an individual to whom section 1291(a) of title 50, appendix, applies;2See References in Text note below.2

children.label: x
children.text: an employee of a Federal land bank, a Federal intermediate credit bank, or a bank for cooperatives;

children.label: xi
children.text: an employee whose pay is fixed and adjusted from time to time in accordance with prevailing rates under subchapter IV of chapter 53 of this title, or by a wage board or similar administrative authority serving the same purpose, except as provided by section 5544 or 5550b of this title;

children.label: xii
children.text: an employee of the Transportation Corps of the Army on a vessel operated by the United States, a vessel employee of the Environmental Science Services Administration, or a vessel employee of the Department of the Interior;

children.label: xiii
children.text: a “teacher” or an individual holding a “teaching position” as defined by ;section 901 of title 20

children.label: xiv
children.text: a Foreign Service officer;

children.label: xv
children.text: a member of the Senior Foreign Service;

children.label: xvi
children.text: member of the Senior Executive Service; or

children.label: xvii
children.text: a member of the Federal Bureau of Investigation and Drug Enforcement Administration Senior Executive Service; and

children.label: A
children.text: is a law enforcement officer within the meaning of section 8331(20) or 8401(17);

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an employee who holds a supervisory or administrative position and is subject to subchapter III of chapter 83, but who does not qualify to be considered a law enforcement officer within the meaning of section 8331(20), would so qualify if such employee had transferred directly to such position after serving as a law enforcement officer within the meaning of such section;

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of an employee who holds a supervisory or administrative position and is subject to chapter 84, but who does not qualify to be considered a law enforcement officer within the meaning of section 8401(17), would so qualify if such employee had transferred directly to such position after performing duties described in section 8401(17)(A) and (B) for at least 3 years; and

children.label: i
children.text: holds a position that the Office of Personnel Management determines would satisfy subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) if the employee were subject to subchapter III of chapter 83 or chapter 84; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is a special agent in the Diplomatic Security Service.
